short_name: ERP compression signals 15-25% equity drawdown within 12 months
full_statement: The equity risk premium has compressed to 0.17%, far below the historical average of 3-5%. This level of valuation stretch has preceded every major drawdown in the past 50 years. With earnings yields barely exceeding Treasury yields, the margin of safety is negligible. A catalyst -- whether rising rates, earnings disappointment, or credit tightening -- should trigger a 15-25% correction in the S&P 500 within 12 months as capital reallocates from equities to bonds and alternatives.
mechanism: ERP compression below 1% -> negligible compensation for equity risk -> capital reallocation trigger -> drawdown as positioning unwinds
prediction: SPY -15% to -25% over next 12 months. TLT +5-15% as flight to quality. GLD +10-20% as alternative store of value.

hard_falsifiers.condition: S&P 500 earnings yield rises above 6% through earnings growth rather than price decline
ERP expands above 3% without a drawdown (re-rating higher)

short_name: Large-cap to small-cap rotation as QQQ/IWM ratio mean-reverts
full_statement: QQQ/IWM ratio at 2.32 represents extreme concentration premium. Historical mean-reversion of this ratio occurs through small-cap outperformance during late-cycle phases. Expect IWM to outperform QQQ by 10-20% over the next 6-12 months as valuation compression hits mega-cap tech disproportionately.
mechanism: Extreme concentration premium -> diminishing marginal returns from mega-cap -> rotation capital seeks smaller-cap value -> IWM outperforms as QQQ/IWM ratio mean-reverts
prediction: IWM outperforms QQQ by 10-20% over next 6-12 months

hard_falsifiers.condition: QQQ/IWM ratio rises above 2.8 (concentration increases further)

soft_falsifiers.condition: Mega-cap tech capex grows 30%+ sustaining narrative

short_name: MP3 transition drives gold to outperform nominal bonds 25-40%
full_statement: The long-term debt cycle is in late MP2 territory with debt-to-GDP above 120%. Interest expense is consuming an increasing share of federal revenue, forcing implicit monetization. Gold and real assets outperform nominal bonds by 25-40% over the next 12 months as the transition to MP3 (monetary financing) accelerates. This is structural, not cyclical -- the arithmetic is unavoidable.
mechanism: Debt accumulation beyond productive capacity -> interest expense spiral -> implicit monetization via deficit spending -> currency debasement -> hard assets outperform nominal claims
prediction: GLD +25-40% vs TLT -5-15% over next 12 months. GLDM as alternate expression.

short_name: Treasury term premium expansion forces long-duration underperformance
full_statement: With federal interest expense exceeding $1T annualized and no credible path to deficit reduction, the term premium on long-duration Treasuries must expand. TLT underperforms short-duration by 10-15% over 6-12 months as the market prices in persistent fiscal dominance and the impossibility of sustained high real rates.
mechanism: Deficit spending -> issuance pressure -> term premium expansion -> long-duration underperformance -> yield curve steepening
prediction: TLT -10% to -15% vs SHY roughly flat over next 6-12 months

hard_falsifiers.condition: Federal deficit falls below $500B annualized through spending cuts
Term premium contracts despite rising issuance (flight to safety overwhelms supply)

soft_falsifiers.name: Recession materializes
soft_falsifiers.condition: NBER declares recession, triggering flight to long-duration safety

short_name: Stretched valuations plus debt monetization favors gold over equities
full_statement: The combination of extreme equity valuation compression (ERP 0.17%) and late-stage debt cycle dynamics (debt-to-GDP >120%) creates a specific prediction: when the equity drawdown materializes, gold benefits from both the flight-to-safety rotation AND the ongoing monetary debasement. GLD outperforms SPY by 30-50% over the next 12 months. This combination is more falsifiable than either theory alone because it requires both mechanisms to be operative simultaneously.
mechanism: ERP compression sets up drawdown risk (valuation) + debt monetization devalues nominal claims (debt cycle) -> gold benefits from both channels simultaneously -> GLD massively outperforms SPY
prediction: GLD outperforms SPY by 30-50% over next 12 months

hard_falsifiers.condition: ERP expands above 3% WITHOUT a drawdown (markets re-rate higher)
Debt-to-GDP falls below 100% AND real rates stay above 2%

short_name: Late-expansion credit cycle signals defensive positioning in HYG
full_statement: The short-term debt cycle shows late-expansion characteristics: HY spreads at 317bp are below long-term averages, unemployment at 4.4% is near cycle lows, and ISM at 48 suggests manufacturing contraction. When the credit cycle turns, HY spreads widen 200-400bp and HYG declines 8-15%. Position SHORT HYG as a hedge against credit cycle turn.
mechanism: Late-expansion complacency -> credit quality deterioration at margin -> trigger event (payroll weakness, earnings miss) -> spread widening -> HYG decline
prediction: HYG -8% to -15% as HY spreads widen to 500-700bp over 6-12 months

hard_falsifiers.condition: HY spreads tighten below 250bp with accelerating GDP growth
ISM rises above 55 with payrolls accelerating above 250K/month
